div.h2: Memory Layout: sha256_padded_t — Two-Block Example (56-byte input)

div.p: Padding Length Formula (Step 5) used = len + 1 = 56 + 1 = 57 total_padded = ⌈(used + 8) / 64⌉ × 64 = ⌈(57 + 8) / 64⌉ × 64 = ⌈65 / 64⌉ × 64 = 2 × 64 = 128 bytes
Boundary Rule: len ≤ 55 → 1 block (55+1+0+8 = 64) len ≥ 56 → 2 blocks (56+1+7+56+8 = 128)
sha256_get_block(padded, i) Returns: padded→data + (i × 64) Block 0: &data[0] = 0x000 offset Block 1: &data[64] = 0x040 offset O(1) pointer arithmetic · no copy · cache-line aligned
Color Legend 🟦 Blue = Data (message bytes) 🟪 Purple = Control (0x80 marker) ⬜ Green = Zero padding / Free 🟠 Orange = Length field / Metadata
sizeof = 128 bytes (data) + sizeof(size_t) Stack-allocated · No heap · Flat contiguous array

num_blocks (at offset sizeof(data) = 128) Type: size_t · Size: 8 bytes (64-bit) Value: 2 = total_padded(128) / SHA256_BLOCK_SIZE(64) Set by: sha256_pad() Step 7 Used by: compression loop bound i < padded->num_blocks Color: 🟠 Metadata
bytes 0x00–0x37 (0–55) Field: Message bytes (verbatim copy) Size: 56 bytes Value: original input data Color: 🟦 Data FIPS: §5.1.1 step 1
byte 0x38 (56) Field: Padding marker Size: 1 byte Value: 0x80 = 1000 0000₂ "1 bit" separator (MSB=1, rest=0) Color: 🟪 Header/Control FIPS: §5.1.1 step 2

bytes 0x39–0x3F (57–63) Field: Zero fill — partial (block 0) Size: 7 bytes Value: 0x00 × 7 Insufficient space for 8-byte length field → overflows to Block 1 Color: ⬜ Padding FIPS: §5.1.1 step 3 (k zero bits)
bytes 0x40–0x77 (64–119) Field: Zero fill — continuation (block 1) Size: 56 bytes Value: 0x00 × 56 Color: ⬜ Padding FIPS: §5.1.1 step 3 (k zero bits cont.)

bytes 0x78–0x7F (120–127) Field: 64-bit big-endian length field Size: 8 bytes (mandatory slot) Value: bit_length = 56 × 8 = 448 = 0x01C0 ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │ 0x78: 0x00 (L >> 56) │ │ 0x79: 0x00 (L >> 48) │ │ 0x7A: 0x00 (L >> 40) │ │ 0x7B: 0x00 (L >> 32) │ │ 0x7C: 0x00 (L >> 24) │ │ 0x7D: 0x00 (L >> 16) │ │ 0x7E: 0x01 (L >> 8) ← MSB │ │ 0x7F: 0xC0 (L >> 0) ← LSB │ └─────────────────────────────────┘ Big-endian: write_be64(buf+120, 448) NOT memcpy — x86 is little-endian! Color: 🟠 Pointer/Length (orange) FIPS: §5.1.1 step 4 "64-bit repr of l"
